minion.description: Weak adversaries meant to be defeated quickly, often in groups
minion.typical_features: Few features, Group Attack, Minion (defeated in one successful hit)

standard.description: Baseline adversaries that provide moderate challenge
standard.typical_features: Balanced mix of offense and utility

bruiser.typical_features: High damage output, lower attack modifiers, simple tactics

leader.description: Adversaries that command and coordinate others
leader.typical_features: Command abilities, troop summoning, tactical bonuses

solo.description: Single powerful adversaries designed to face entire parties
solo.typical_features: Multiple actions per turn, Relentless, Phase Changes

horde.description: Large groups that weaken as they take damage
horde.typical_features: Horde feature (damage decreases with HP loss), area effects

ranged.typical_features: Higher attack modifiers, ranged attacks, positioning abilities

social.typical_features: Social manipulation, information gathering, non-combat resolution

support.typical_features: Healing, buffs, environmental control, low damage

hit_points_guidelines.1: Single successful hit defeats, no damage thresholds needed (Minion)
hit_points_guidelines.2: Defeated in one hit, definitely down in two (requires Major threshold only)
hit_points_guidelines.3: Could be defeated in one big hit, few hits otherwise
hit_points_guidelines.4-6: Safest range for lasting a few hits without dragging
hit_points_guidelines.7-9: Tougher adversaries that last the whole fight

damage_allocation_by_type.hordes: d8s or d10s initially, reduce to d4s/d6s when damaged
damage_allocation_by_type.leaders: d10s if combat-focused, d8s if command-focused
damage_allocation_by_type.minions: Flat damage instead of dice pools
damage_allocation_by_type.ranged: d8s or d10s - slightly higher damage

attack_modifier_adjustments.increase_by_1to4: For adversaries that should hit more often
attack_modifier_adjustments.decrease_by_1to4: For adversaries with especially powerful attacks

common_features.name: Momentum
common_features.description: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
common_features.balance_note: Creates more action opportunities for GM

common_features.name: Relentless (X)
common_features.description: This adversary can be spotlighted up to X times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.
common_features.balance_note: Allows single adversary to compete with full party

common_features.name: Slow
common_features.description: When you spotlight this adversary and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token and describe preparation. Next spotlight with token allows action.
common_features.balance_note: Telegraphs dangerous attacks, gives players reaction time

common_features.name: Terrifying
common_features.description: When this adversary makes a successful attack, all PCs within Close range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.
common_features.balance_note: More powerful than Momentum - don't combine both

common_features.name: Group Attack
common_features.description: Multiple minions can combine attacks for increased effectiveness
common_features.balance_note: Makes minion swarms threatening despite individual weakness

common_features.name: Horde
common_features.description: Damage decreases as the horde takes damage, representing loss of numbers
common_features.balance_note: Creates diminishing threat curve as battle progresses

limitations.resource_costs: Most common - Stress or Fear costs limit usage
limitations.action_reaction_rolls: Require successful rolls or grant targets reaction rolls
limitations.usage_limits: Once per rest, once per session, or vault placement
limitations.tokens: Add/spend tokens to track limited uses

fear_features.design_rule: Features that cost Fear shouldn't generate Fear (avoid feedback loops)
fear_features.scene_impact: Should create turning points or reversals

cognitive_load.reaction_limit: Avoid too many reactions - heaviest GM mental load

feature_count_by_type.solos: Most features but manageable as single adversary (4-8 total)

tier_1_bandit.name: Bandit
tier_1_bandit.description: A desperate outlaw who has turned to highway robbery.

passives.name: Opportunist
passives.description: Gains advantage on attacks against Vulnerable targets.

actions.name: Desperate Strike
actions.description: Make an attack with advantage, but become Vulnerable until your next turn.

tier_2_assassin_poisoner.name: Assassin Poisoner
tier_2_assassin_poisoner.description: A cunning scoundrel skilled in both poisons and ambushing.

passives.name: Grindletooth Venom
passives.description: Targets who mark HP from the Assassin's attacks are Vulnerable until they clear a HP.

passives.name: Out of Nowhere
passives.description: The Assassin has advantage on attacks if they are Hidden.

actions.name: Fumigation
actions.description: Drop a smoke bomb that fills the air within Close range with smoke, Dizzying all targets in this area. Dizzied targets have disadvantage on their next action roll, then clear the condition.

tier_3_ogre_bruiser.name: Ogre Bruiser
tier_3_ogre_bruiser.description: A massive, simple-minded giant with tremendous physical strength.

actions.name: Ground Slam
actions.description: Make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Successful hits knock targets prone.

reactions.name: Momentum
reactions.trigger: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC
